moves to a randomly chosen empty adjacent location that is within the grid. If there is no empty adjacent
location that is within the grid, the Grasshopper does not move, but turns 45 degrees to the right without
changing its location. Which method(s) of the Bug class should the Grasshopper class override so that a Grasshopper can
behave as described above? I.
act() II.
move() III.
canMove()

text:
A car dealership needs a program to store information about the cars for sale. For
each car, they want to keep track of the following information: number of doors(2 or 4), whether the car has air conditioning, and its average number of miles
per gallon. Which of the following is the best object-oriented program design?

options.A: Use 1 class, Car, with 3 instance variables: int numDoors, boolean hasAir, and double milesPerGallon.
options.B: Use 4 unrelated classes: Car, Doors, AirConditioning, and MilesPerGallon.
options.C: Use a class Car with 3 subclasses: Doors, AirConditioning, and MilesPerGallon.
options.D: Use a class Car, with a subclass Doors, with a subclass AirConditioning, with a subclass MilesPerGallon.
options.E: Use 3 classes: Doors, AirConditioning, and MilesPerGallon, each with a subclass Car.

text:
The method findLongest does not work as intended. Which of the following
best describes the value returned by a call to findLongest ?

options.A: It is the length of the shortest consecutive block of the value target in nums.
options.B: It is the length of the array nums.
options.C: It is the number of occurrences of the value target in nums.
options.D: It is the length of the first consecutive block of the value target in nums.
options.E: It is the length of the last consecutive block of the value target in nums.

group_context: Questions11–12 refer to the following information. Consider the following instance variable nums and method findLongest with line numbers added for reference. Method findLongest is intended to find the longest consecutive block of the value target occurring in the array nums; however, findLongest does not work as intended. For example, if the array nums contains the values[7, 10, 10, 15, 15, 15, 15, 10, 10, 10, 15, 10, 10], the call findLongest(10) should return 3, the length of the longest consecutive block of 10s. 28 © 2014 The College Board.

options.C: Insert the statement `lenCount = 0;` between lines 10 and 11.
options.D: Insert the statement `lenCount = 0;` between lines 11 and 12.
options.E: Insert the statement `lenCount = 0;` between lines 12 and 13.

Method findLongest is intended to find the longest consecutive block of the value target occurring in the array nums; however, findLongest does not work as intended. For example, if the array nums contains the values[7, 10, 10, 15, 15, 15, 15, 10, 10, 10, 15, 10, 10], the call findLongest(10) should return 3, the length of the longest consecutive block of 10s. 28 © 2014 The College Board.
